title: Irrigation Cost Check
description: Industrial-grade tool to evaluate irrigation system cost efficiency, hidden losses, and actionable improvements based on ISO 14046, Lean Six Sigma, and WERC DC metrics.

inputs.label: Annual water volume used
inputs.businessContext: Total volume of water withdrawn for irrigation per year.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total volume of water withdrawn for irrigation per year.

inputs.label: Water cost per m³
inputs.businessContext: Unit cost of water including extraction, treatment, and delivery.

businessContext_i18n.en: Unit cost of water including extraction, treatment, and delivery.

inputs.label: Energy cost per kWh
inputs.businessContext: Average electricity cost for pumping and distribution.

businessContext_i18n.en: Average electricity cost for pumping and distribution.

inputs.label: Pump system efficiency
inputs.businessContext: Overall efficiency of pump and motor system (ISO 9906).

businessContext_i18n.en: Overall efficiency of pump and motor system (ISO 9906).

inputs.label: Distribution losses
inputs.businessContext: Percentage of water lost due to leaks, evaporation, and runoff.

businessContext_i18n.en: Percentage of water lost due to leaks, evaporation, and runoff.

inputs.label: Annual labor hours for irrigation
inputs.businessContext: Total labor hours dedicated to irrigation operation and maintenance.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total labor hours dedicated to irrigation operation and maintenance.

inputs.label: Annual maintenance cost
inputs.businessContext: Total annual cost for repairs, parts, and servicing.

businessContext_i18n.en: Total annual cost for repairs, parts, and servicing.

rules.message: Pump efficiency must be between 10% and 100%.
Distribution losses must be between 0% and 50%.
Water volume must be greater than zero.
Irrigated area must be greater than zero.
Drip systems are strongly recommended to have automation for optimal efficiency.

thresholds.message: Water cost exceeds $1.00/m³ – consider water conservation measures.
Pump efficiency below 60% – immediate maintenance or replacement recommended.
Distribution losses above 20% – investigate leaks and improve system.
Labor hours exceed 1000/year – consider automation to reduce costs.

suggestedActions: Review inputs and verify results against site standards.
